name: GPU transcription throughput without quality loss

description: Baseline GPU throughput benchmark and harness setup. Added autoresearch.md, autoresearch.sh, a dedicated CUDA benchmark binary, and deterministic transcript references for short and repeated workloads. Current baseline is 924.299ms for the 60-second repeated fixture with short_exact=1, long_exact=1, and short_keyword_hits=6.

Tried increasing the transcription chunk size from 30s to 120s to reduce repeated prompt and chunk overhead on the GPU. It improved wall_ms substantially, but long_exact dropped from 1 to 0 because the 60-second repeated workload lost one repeated segment in the transcript, so this is a real quality regression and must be rejected.

Tried eliminating tiny tail chunks by folding a sub-1s remainder back into earlier chunks, which reduced the 60-second synthetic workload from three chunks to two and improved wall_ms. The speedup was not acceptable because long_exact fell to 0: shifting the chunk boundary changed the repeated transcript ('tsunzhu' -> 'tsunzuo' and other formatting differences), so this chunk-boundary optimization is not quality preserving.

Tried replacing CUDA flash-attn with a manual matmul/softmax attention path for single-token decode steps, hoping to cut kernel-launch overhead during generation. It was much slower in practice and also changed the long repeated transcript (long_exact=0), so the CUDA decode path should stay on flash-attn.

Optimized mel extraction by precomputing each mel filter's non-zero FFT-bin range and skipping zero-weight bins during filterbank accumulation. This preserves the exact short and long transcripts while improving the primary GPU benchmark from 924.299ms to 861.699ms (~6.8% faster, 69.65x realtime).

Tried precomputing FFT bit-reversal indices to remove the branchy per-frame permutation math in the custom mel FFT. Quality stayed exact, but the result (889.511ms) was still slower than the current kept mel-range optimization at 861.699ms, so this extra complexity is not worth keeping.
